question.text: Which of the following is an advantage of having multiple nominees across different financial accounts?

answer.text: It eliminates the need for a Will
It ensures quick and convenient transfer of specific assets to designated persons upon death
It guarantees that assets are distributed as per the deceased's wishes
It provides tax benefits to the nominee

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Multiple nominations across accounts ensure quick transfer of specific assets to designated persons, avoiding delays in accessing funds. However, nominees hold assets as trustees, not as owners. **The Trap:** Option A is wrong — nominations do NOT replace a Will. Option C overstates the role of nominations — they don't guarantee distribution as per wishes; only a Will does that. **Cross-Exam Context:** Nomination across multiple financial products is tested in Series V-A (MF), II-A/II-B (RTA), and XVII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise clients to nominate in ALL financial accounts (bank, MF, demat, NPS, insurance, PPF) and review annually. Ensure all nominations are consistent with their Will.
